REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS EILEEN DOHERTY AND MATTHEW NICHOLS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. THIS DEMURRER WAS SUBMITTED FOR FILING ON APRIL 29, 2022. PAPERS SUBMITTED INCLUDED A 3-PAGE NOTICE OF DEMURRER; A 3-PAGE DEMURRER AND A 42-PAGE DECLARATION. NO POINTS AND AUTHORITIES SUBMITTED FOR FILING. ON JUNE 13, 2022 MOVING PARTY FILED POINTS AND AUTHORITIES. ON JULY 29, 2022 THE COURT PROVIDED THE MOVING PARTY AN OPPORTUNITY TO EXPLAIN THE LATE-FILING. MOVING PARTY APPEARS TO SUGGEST THAT JUNE 13, 2022 FILING WAS A "COURTESY COPY." A COURTESY COPY IS A TRUE AND CORRECT HARD COPY OF A DOCUMENT PREVIOUSLY/SIMULTANEOUSLY SUBMITTED FOR FILING PROVIDED TO THE DEPARTMENT PER SFRL 2.7B. NO EXPLANATION PROVIDED FOR THE LATE FILING. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED (D501)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-06-2023 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-13-2022 Set For Trial On Jul-10-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-02-2022 Continued To Oct-26-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-12-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs EILEEN DOHERTY and MATTHEW NICHOLS' DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is off calendar. This demurrer was submitted for filing on April 29, 2022. Papers submitted included a 3-page Notice of Demurrer; a 3-page demurrer and a 42-page declaration. No points and authorities submitted for filing. On June 13, 2022 moving party filed points and authorities. On July 29, 2022 the Court provided the moving party an opportunity to explain the late-filing. Moving party appears to suggest that June 13, 2022 filing was a "courtesy copy." A courtesy copy is a true and correct hard copy of a document previously/simultaneously submitted for filing provided to the department per SFRL 2.7B. No explanation provided for the late filing.
Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not reported (D501)

Defendants Eileen Doherty Individually And As Trustee Of The Doherty Bypass Trust Dated October 1 2015 And Matthew Nichols Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-12-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs EILEEN DOHERTY and MATTHEW NICHOLS' Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint is DENIED. Complaint sufficiently alleges facts to support conscious disregard of Plaintiff's rights/malice. Request for oral testimony is Denied. No offer of proof provide and no explanation given for an evidentiary hearing on a motion challenging the sufficiency of allegations in the complaint. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not reported (D501)
